description_long: This imposing gatehouse is constructed from tan-colored stone blocks, featuring a central arched gateway flanked by two cylindrical turrets with battlements on top. The main entryway is accentuated by a tall, pointed arch and a pair of large, vertically ribbed wooden doors. Decorative elements, such as painted shields or banners, adorn the facade on either side of the arch, adding a touch of heraldic character. The structure extends horizontally with crenellated walls on both sides, each punctuated by small, narrow slits resembling arrow loops. The rear of the gatehouse is simpler, with the stonework continuing and the turrets visible but lacking the decorative front details. The overall design evokes a fortified medieval style, suggesting both defensive and ceremonial functions.
description: A tan stone gatehouse with a central arched entry, flanked by cylindrical turrets and crenellated walls, featuring decorative shields and a large wooden door.
description_view_0: A narrow side profile showing the cylindrical turret and the thickness of the gatehouse wall.
description_view_1: The detailed front view displays the arched entryway, decorative shields, twin turrets, and crenellated walls.
description_view_2: The opposite side profile, mirroring the first, highlights the turret and wall thickness.
description_view_3: The rear view reveals the plain stonework, turrets, and the back of the central gate without decorative elements.
attribute_entryway_design: Tall arched stone doorway with large wooden double doors and decorative shields.
attribute_window_placement: Narrow vertical arrow slits along the walls and turrets.
